intro: Migrating C# source to procedural C requires stripping away the entire .NET runtime, garbage collection infrastructure, object-oriented paradigms, and type safety layers. Developers must transition to bare-metal systems programming, manually managing memory layouts, raw pointer manipulation, and platform-specific compilation constraints. This structural downshift guarantees absolute control and minimal hardware footprints at the expense of developer velocity and safety guarantees.

diffs.from: Static, nominal object-oriented environment providing reflection, reified generics, and runtime type tracking.
diffs.to: Weak, static procedural typing system supporting raw typecasting, explicit structural alignment, and zero runtime type metadata.

diffs.from: High-level object design supporting structural classes, automated properties, lambda closures, and integrated namespaces.
diffs.to: Low-level imperative block configuration using bare structs, global function implementations, macro definitions, and header inclusions.

diffs.from: Completely automated allocation framework managed by a multi-generation runtime heap garbage collection engine.
diffs.to: Manual execution control requiring precise calls to malloc/calloc/free, explicit stack sizing, and raw pointer management.

diffs.from: High-level Task Parallel Library scheduling multi-threaded data mutations safely via built-in async/await constructs.
diffs.to: Low-level operating system threads (POSIX pthreads or Windows API threads), raw mutex locks, and manual state synchronization controls.

diffs.from: Comprehensive .NET base framework containing high-level web tools, cryptographic objects, and deep relational query layers.
diffs.to: Minimal system wrapper set (libc) providing fundamental memory operations, low-level file I/O streams, and basic math routines.

examples.title: Dynamic Data Container Allocation
examples.description: Compares an automated C# object lifecycle with manual dynamic memory allocation and clean-up execution in C.

examples.title: Structural String Comparison
examples.description: Contrasts object string instance value checks in C# with raw character buffer calculations via standard library tools in C.

tips.title: Track All Allocated Pointer References
tips.body: C has no runtime protection against memory leaks or out-of-bounds corruption. Every single `malloc` call must be explicitly paired with a corresponding `free` statement across all code execution branches.

tips.title: Replace Classes with Structs and Pointers
tips.body: Deconstruct C# class models into standard flat C `struct` types. Emulate object methodology by passing an explicit instance pointer reference (conventionally named `this` or `self`) as the first argument of functions.

tips.title: Enforce String Null-Termination
tips.body: C# strings track character length safely inside class memory wrappers. C strings are bare byte arrays requiring a trailing null character (`\0`) to terminate functions; always allocate an extra byte for this marker.

tips.title: Implement Manual Error Signatures
tips.body: C completely lacks structured exception handling (`try/catch`). Rewrite error workflows to return integer error codes or status enums from functions, and pass output payloads back through dedicated pointer parameters.
